text: Achieving high-energy-density cathode materials with robust structural integrity against the fast intercalation and deintercalation of sodium ions remains a major hurdle for sodium-ion batteries (SIBs). In this work, we explore a new strategy to develop a P2-layered oxide cathode material with the formula of Na₀.₇Li₀.₀₃[Mg₀.₁₅Li₀.₀₇Mn₀.₇₅]O₂. Through combining density functional theory (DFT) simulations and experiments, the lithium dual-site substitution with Li at the transition-metal (TM) and alkali-metal (AM) sites not only introduces the Na−O−Li electronic configurations benefited to the activation of anionic oxygen redox, leading to the increase in specific capacity, but also enhances the structural integrity of the material by using Li ions in AM sites of AM to act as the rigid LiO₆ prismatic pillars, which prohibit undesirable phase transitions in both high and low charge states. Therefore, the Na₀.₇Li₀.₀₃[Mg₀.₁₅Li₀.₀₇Mn₀.₇₅]O₂ exhibits a specific capacity of 266 mAh g⁻¹ with only a slight volume expansion of only 1.2% within a wide operating voltage window ranging from 1.5 to 4.6 V. This study clearly shows that the achieved battery performance demonstrates that the dual-site substitution could successfully eliminate the trade-off between high capacity and structural integrity.
